candidates.stmt:
I am proud to be standing as the Green Party Candidate in Broadwater, because I am passionate about making our community and town and greener, safer and fairer place for everyone. As someone who has lived in Worthing my whole life, I care so much about protecting our community and I want to ensure that our local area will continue to thrive for future generations.

Protecting our local environment and green spaces is incredibly important to me. I want to make sure residents have access to clean, safe and welcoming places where they can spend time with friends and family. That means protecting our beautiful parks from litter and anti-social behaviour as well as looking after our wonderful beach and sea and standing up to those pumping sewage into our waters.

I’m also passionate about improving road safety, supporting more social and genuinely affordable housing and strengthening the community spirit and diversity in Worthing. I also want to see more young people in politics who can bring in newer, fresh ideas and provide real change and real hope in Worthing

I have worked and lived in the Worthing Area for the last 25 years.  For the last 10 years I have given all my free time to serving the local community, including 8 years as a Worthing Borough Councillor. As a councillor I was honoured to serve as the Worthing Mayor for 2 years and helped guide the town through the difficult covid period.

I was also the Borough’s Armed Forces Champion for 3 years, supporting our local service personnel and Veterans, this is a role I am proud to have held as a veteran of the Royal Navy myself. I continue to support our Armed Forces Community today, holding several positions with local associations. This is a major reason for me wanting to stand as your Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for the Reform Party in the East Worthing and Shoreham constituency.

Only Reform UK has clear fully costed policies to protect and strengthen our Armed Forces and Veteran Community, protecting our borders by controlled immigration, and safeguarding our children by banning Transgender ideology being pushed in our schools.

The Conservatives are tired and have run out of ideas, the Labour Party simply has no idea, and, as we have seen in Worthing, would not stand up for ordinary people, or our traditions, instead opting to rewrite history to suit the fringe minority groups.

It is time for our Country to have a Fresh Start, and only the Reform UK Party can deliver that, so please lend me your vote to enable all of us to thrive.

Soraya has actively served Worthing’s community since 2018, using food to gather people in the name of social justice and cultural education.  Soraya is also Worthing Green Party’s Diversity and Inclusion Officer and has spoken publicly on various issues in Worthing at Palestine marches, racism vigils and unity rallies. Having lived in Worthing her whole life, Soraya’s pledge as future Green Councillor for Castle ward is 1) to commit to building a supportive, unified & cohesive community, 2) to ensure all people are fairly represented in all spaces, and 3) to do the very best for the human rights and well-being of all citizens of this town.

Natasha grew up in Worthing, and studied at Davison High School and Worthing Sixth Form, before leaving to study and work internationally. She holds a doctorate from Oxford and has spent her career working in business strategy and international development, including improving livelihoods for smallholder farmers and expanding access to medicines in low-income countries.

Now back home, Natasha is standing for Central Ward on Worthing Borough Council and Pier Division on West Sussex County Council. She brings practical experience of working on issues that matter to real people, and the independence to speak up for Worthing residents without fear or favour.

I have lived and worked in Worthing for the last 40 years. I am a passionate believer in a complete and full-blooded Brexit. The UK should be and remain an independent sovereign nation. I believe the UK should be self-sufficient in all essential goods and services.

In practise, this includes exploiting to the the full ALL our natural resources such as coal, shale gas, and North Sea oil. As a sovereign nation, we should not be forced to accept the jurisdiction of ANY foreign courts. I believe the boats arriving illegally from France should be sent straight back.

As for legal immigration, I believe in ‘net zero’; one in, one out, selecting only those with training beneficial to this country. I believe in low taxes; two unchangeable biological sexes; no political or sexual indoctrination in schools, colleges or universities; no wokery; Law & Order. Basically, everything old school Tories used to believe in, but clearly don’t now.

If, like me you think that the Tories have betrayed us for the last 14 years, but you can’t stomach voting for Starmer’s Labour, then I’m your man! Vote for me!

Vote for Reform UK!

My name is Bradley Rowatt and I’m standing to be your Conservative councillor on the 7th of May.

Our priorities are to benefit the lives of those in Durrington and Worthing as a whole - which Labour has forgotten both nationally and  locally

Firstly, I will stand against any housing of illegal migrants. Worthing will NOT become another dumping ground like Crowborough.

Secondly, I will stand against decline - Our pavements and roads have been neglected for over four years causing them to now become a blight on our town.

Thirdly, I will support efforts to crack down on crime. Shoplifting is now running rampant in Worthing. I will work with our PCC - Katy Bourne - to punish repeat offenders to bring back safety in our streets.

I will fight for lower council tax and bring an end to labours CHRONIC overspending.

IF YOU AGREE WITH ME. Vote Bradley on the 7th of May.

I became a member of the Green Party because I strongly believe in equality, community and the environment.

I have lived in Worthing for 13 years, working locally in health and social care. I currently work in the NHS as an Occupational therapist.

I previously worked in the homeless sector and  have seen first hand the impact of the lack of housing for local people- families and people with disabilities living in temporary accommodation out of area away from their families and social networks. We need to do more for our people unfortunately experiencing unstable housing.

I am also passionate about building community spaces in Worthing. I was a volunteer director and fundraiser for the alternative arts venue and social enterprise Cellar Arts Club. I also coordinated and hosted music events One Love Worthing, celebrating diversity and community support groups.

Key Issues I would focus on as a councillor:

- Housing for all – holding developers to account
- Community to build unity and opportunity
- Community wealth development- sourcing investment locally to build our economy.

Hello I’m Jasmine, I live here in Worthing with my family. I am passionate about Worthing, its people, and protecting what makes our community special. I’m incredibly proud to be standing for Goring Ward with the Green Party.

Worthing Green Party’s priorities are clear: clean water, safer roads, and genuinely affordable housing. These are the issues residents raise time and again, and where we know we can make a real difference. Alongside this, I would love to see a safe, well-designed cycle infrastructure that works for everyone — something I hear strongly from fellow cyclists and from those who joined our brilliant Kidical Mass ride which brought together over 100 cyclists for a fun, positive, and unifying community event. I am an avid cyclist myself but as a car driver for work, I fully understand how important cars are in daily life. That’s why I also strongly support improving public transport, giving people more practical and sustainable alternatives.

I’ve already been actively involved in local campaigning. A friend and I worked to prevent the closure of the Barrington Road footpath during the Bellway Homes Queen Elizabeth development. After listening carefully to residents, we pushed for a wide, safe path with proper drainage and lighting, while also protecting the rich diversity of local flora and fauna. We organised a petition, brought residents’ voices to planning meetings, and successfully secured many of these improvements. Clare and Ian have continued this work, ensuring trees and hedging are replanted.

I’m proud to be campaigning alongside Clare and Ian, who have already done so much for Goring. This is a crucial Borough election, and the Green Party is in a strong position. The more Green Councillors we elect, the more we can deliver for our communities – and we need to see Ian representing us at West Sussex County Council.

As a Senior Community Development Worker, I listen to the needs of people from all walks of life, helping to build more conn

I’ve lived in Worthing for 30 years, my children were born and grew up here and I’m passionate about regenerating our town to its’ former vibrancy.  Having managed complex international projects as Human Resources Director, I want to use those organisation, planning and people engagement skills to ensure we have adequate affordable housing, safer, cleaner roads and beaches to be proud of.  We’ve been out on the doorstep in recent months, and it’s clear that people want a sense of community and belonging, to feel connected to each other and involved in deciding how public money gets spent, how everyday life in Worthing is organised.  I promise to listen well, work hard, and respond quickly and honestly.  A vote for Green is a vote to make hope normal again.

Martin McCabe was born and raised in Worthing, Martin's goals are simple: to empower our community and demand a better politics that puts people first. A Building Engineer and lecturer, Martin is obsessed with making sure that politicians get the details right.

Martin has a strong record of standing up for local people. In 2018, he helped a local special needs charity find a new home so its work could continue. He has raised funds for local disability and women's football teams and campaigned for a People's Vote on Brexit. In May 2019, he is running the 100km London to Brighton ultra marathon to raise money for West Sussex Mind. Martin is the Chairman of the Adur and Worthing Liberal Democrats and our candidate for Tarring ward in the 2019 Worthing Council Elections.

Having spent many years working in the private sector, including senior management roles I bring a strong track record of leadership, accountability, and delivery. 
I care deeply about our local community and bring energy, enthusiasm, and a proactive approach to delivering the real change residents are calling for. People deserve a council that listens, takes action, and puts local priorities first.
My top priorities are:
•	Revitalising the town centre, celebrating local heritage, and protecting small businesses
•	Improving the condition of our roads
•	Tackling crime, antisocial behaviour, and shoplifting
•	Protecting our coastline and safeguarding green spaces
•	Managing major projects responsibly and ensuring value for money in council spending

Only Reform UK is offering the bold change needed locally. 
Labour tried to take away your democratic right to vote - Lend me your vote to make real positive changes for all of Worthing.

I have been living in West Worthing for eight years and am passionate about the local area and what it has to offer the local residents – if only it was cared for by the people in charge of building its fairer communities, keeping its water supplies clean, fixing the potholes, and keeping Worthing a safe town for future generations to grow up in. As someone who has two jobs and still worries about paying the bills on time every month, I know the challenges and worries of everyday life and will work my hardest to keep this town special and help it grow.

Worthing Borough Councillor for Northbrook ward.
I feel i can make a difference, I aim to carry out civic duty representing the areas residents and make an impact locally
I'm a Married father of 3 who lives in the ward i represent, Northbrook, with My wife.
We have been married for 12 years, together for 15 and even got married in the ward, up at Highdown!
I know that with my Hard work ethic, sincere Dedication, Family Values and self belief, I am able to represent the ward to thrive and unify, our community needs to come together and make life living here better for ourselves

I’m excited to be part of the Green Party’s movement of hope and unity. I will work hard to address issues for Worthing residents and nurture our community. I love living in Worthing, and becoming a mum recently made me look at our town with a fresh outlook. I’m committed to making Worthing a greener, safer and happier place for our children to grow up in.

"

I am Nick Wiltshire, your local Liberal Democrat candidate for Castle Ward. I live and work locally, so I use the same facilities and roads that you do, including pot-holes. Working in healthcare means listening to others, helping others, and working as a team, qualities I would bring to Worthing’s Council, if elected.

I am a passionate environmentalist and want to make Worthing a kinder, greener and more sustainable place – planting more urban trees, recycling more types of plastic, making our transport system greener and ensuring that our bathing waters are clean enough to enjoy for swimming, surfing or sailing!"

[taken from: https://www.wor-ld.org.uk/our-team/nick-wiltshire]

I have lived in the Offington Ward for nearly 30 years during which time I’ve enjoyed being involved with the local community and serving the Findon Valley Residents Association.  At the heart of the Green Party is an acceptance of all people and a desire to give them opportunity to enjoy life while not disadvantaging others.  The Green Party’s aim to create a more sustainable and fairer country by challenging some long held, but not working, approaches is both exciting and long overdue.

I’m proud to call Worthing home. After moving here two years ago, I quickly fell in love with this town, its people, and its potential. As a married woman working in the railway, I understand the importance of reliable services, strong communities, and being listened to.

I’m standing because I want to make Worthing even better, for everyone who lives here.

I am a fierce campaigner for clean waterways. The ongoing dumping of sewage into our sea is unacceptable. Water companies must be held accountable, and their profits should be reinvested into upgrading infrastructure—not lining pockets while our environment suffers.

I also care deeply about our local economy. Worthing thrives when independent businesses thrive. I want to support small, local enterprises and bring more life back to our high streets.

Community matters. Worthing should be a place where everyone feels safe, welcome, and respected. I stand firmly against racism and homophobia and will always champion equality and inclusion.

Beyond politics, I’m passionate about music and the role it plays in bringing people together. I want to see more opportunities for young people in Worthing to access, learn, and enjoy music, because every young person deserves a creative outlet and a sense of belonging.

I’m not just someone who lives in Worthing. I’m someone who wants to fight for it

With nearly a decade of unwavering commitment to the Green movement, I’m standing in East Worthing to offer a beacon of principled politics and genuine hope. Too many people feel unrepresented and unheard, left cold by hateful discourse and divisive rhetoric. I have a degree in Politics and International Relations and have spent years volunteering for causes I believe in. I want to be that alternative for the people of East Worthing — representing the area with compassion, force and an unwavering commitment to those I represent.

After over 30 years of Education and Leadership I want to stand and deliver for the people of Tarring. I strongly believe that we have a duty to look after local people and ensure our council tax is used efficiently to improve services and the area we live in.

Cloe is a Worthing resident whose work and life is rooted in community work. She works in Learning Disabilities and has volunteered for various initiatives such as after school clubs. She believes the people of Tarring deserve better representation. The community in Tarring deserves to have its green spaces protected, functioning and safe roads, support for the vulnerable and someone who uplifts their voices.

Vote for Cloe if you want someone who cares for the community in Tarring.

Tarring’s voice won’t be lost or forgotten with Cloe.
